dataset_description: Frozen snapshot of the USGS real-time feed 'M4.5+ earthquakes, past 30 days', downloaded 2026-06-20 (GeoJSON FeatureCollection). Each feature has properties.mag, properties.place, properties.time (ms epoch UTC), and geometry.coordinates = [lon, lat, depth_km]. This injected snapshot is the authoritative source; the report and interactive map must be built from THIS file, not a fresh live pull.
interactive_requirements: a geographic scatter/map of epicenters using lon/lat (points must land in the correct places, no lon/lat swap, no everything-at-0,0)
a time control (slider or animation) over the ~30-day window that visibly changes which events are shown
a magnitude distribution chart (e.g. histogram of mag)

ground_truth_anchors.note: Computed directly from the injected snapshot. The exact count is fixed by the file; small differences only if the executor filters. Anchors are used to confirm the report read the real data.
ground_truth_anchors.coordinate_convention: geometry.coordinates = [longitude, latitude, depth_km]; longitude first. A map that swaps these will place points in the wrong hemisphere.

expected_analysis_points: Most M4.5+ events cluster along the Pacific Ring of Fire (~80% of events by a rough lon/lat heuristic): west Pacific (Philippines, Indonesia, Japan, Kamchatka) and east Pacific (Andes/Chile, Central America, Alaska)
Mid-ocean ridges (e.g. Mid-Atlantic Ridge) also produce notable events but fewer
The largest event in the window was ~M7.8 near the Philippines
Magnitude distribution is heavily skewed: many events near M4.5-5.0, very few above M6
scoring_notes: Build from the injected GeoJSON. Reward a working interactive HTML whose map points are geographically correct (Ring-of-Fire pattern visible, no coordinate reversal), whose time control actually filters events, and whose magnitude chart matches the data. Penalize fabricated counts, reversed coordinates, a static (non-interactive) page, blank charts, or a report that is generic seismology prose not tied to this snapshot.
dynamic_content_note: The live USGS feed changes daily, but the injected snapshot is frozen and authoritative. Judge against the injected snapshot, not a current pull. If the executor re-downloads live data and the counts differ, that is acceptable only if the methodology is sound and it is clearly noted; prefer the injected snapshot as ground truth.
